utility.waterProvider: City of Temple Utility Services Division
utility.fireFlowNotes: City of Temple maintains municipal water system with fire protection via hydrants and water mains throughout service area. Specific fire flow/hydrant details not published online; contact City for fire flow certification at 254-298-5611 (water/sewer emergencies).
utility.sewerProvider: City of Temple Utility Services Division
utility.tapFees: Water deposit: $115–$2,500 (commercial, based on meter size). Sewer: included in water billing. New account fee: $25. Extra trip fee: $25. Rates effective 10/1/2025: Water (3/4-inch meter, first 2,000 gal) $13.40/mo + $4.85/1,000 gal over minimum. Wastewater minimum (2,000 gal): $25.90/mo + $9.75/1,000 gal over. Customers outside city limits pay 25% higher water rates.

access.vpdSource: Not published online — local street data held by City of Temple Engineering or Bell County
access.nearestSignal: Blackland Road/Canyon Creek Drive (exact distance unknown)

utility.waterProvider: City of McKinney (wholesale source: North Texas Municipal Water District - NTMWD)
utility.fireFlowNotes: McKinney maintains fire hydrants throughout city service area. City of McKinney 24-hour emergency contact (972) 547-7360 can provide specific hydrant/main size info for 3303 N McDonald St. Sprinkler system fire-flow testing required per commercial building code—typically 1,500+ GPM at 20 PSI minimum.
utility.electricProvider: Oncor Electric Delivery (transmission/distribution; deregulated retail market for power purchase)
utility.tapFees: Not published on public website. Contact City of McKinney Water Billing (972) 547-7550 or Development Services for new commercial water/sewer meter tap fees and impact fees. Standard commercial connection includes meter installation fee plus impact/capacity fees (TBD).

access.vpdSource: Not published online — call McKinney Engineering or Collin County for local counts

utility.fireFlowNotes: Not published on city website. Recommend contacting Greenfield Fire Department (317-477-4301) or Water Utility Manager for fire flow analysis at 451 Meek St.
utility.sewerProvider: City of Greenfield Wastewater Utility (service inside city limits only; septic likely required for extraterritorial site)
utility.electricProvider: City of Greenfield Power and Light
utility.tapFees: Water availability fee: $2,000/acre (as of Oct 1, 2021). Water connection (1-inch meter): $4,625. Sewer connection: contact Wastewater Utility (317-477-4360) for rates if available.

access.frontageRoad: W 300 N (also known as County Road 300 North)
access.vpdSource: Not published online — call INDOT Traffic Statistics or Hancock County Engineering
access.nearestSignal: Unknown — requires field verification or contact with Greenfield City or Hancock County Engineering

utility.fireFlowNotes: City of Springboro schedules fire hydrant flushing throughout the year; specific fire flow and main size for Pioneer Blvd location requires contact with city engineering.
utility.sewerProvider: City of Springboro (operated by Veolia North America)
utility.electricProvider: Duke Energy Ohio (distribution); deregulated market for generation
utility.tapFees: Not published; water/sewer rates frozen since Jan 1, 2019 ($13.00/mo + tiered water usage; $34.00/mo + $1.88/1000 gal sewer). New tap/impact fees should be confirmed with City Utility Dept.

access.vpdSource: Not published online — call OKI or ODOT for access

utility.fireFlowNotes: Published fire flow requirements not found in public materials. NKWD maintains three water treatment plants with 54 million gallons per day total capacity. Site is within municipal service area. Recommend contacting NKWD Engineering at 859-578-9898 for fire flow specifications, hydrant locations, and main sizes adjacent to 2150 Harris Pike.
utility.tapFees: Specific new service connection fees not published online. NKWD maintains published rate schedules (Rates, Rules and Regulations document) available at https://www.nkywater.org/DocumentCenter/View/814/Rates-Rules-and-Regulations-PDF. Recommend contacting NKWD directly for current commercial water/sewer tap fees and impact fees.

access.vpdSource: Not published online — KYTC Traffic Count database requires interactive map access
access.nearestSignal: At Harris Pike & Madison Pike (KY 17) intersection — immediate frontage

utility.waterProvider: City of Spring Hill Water Department

waterContact.name: Spring Hill Water General Manager - Dan Allen

utility.fireFlowNotes: Spring Hill Water operates a 4 million GPD treatment plant with 150+ miles of distribution mains and lift stations; municipal system provides adequate fire flow capability. Confirm specific hydrant location and main size with utility engineering at (931) 797-2152 (non-emergency water issues).
utility.sewerProvider: City of Spring Hill Water Reclamation Division (municipal sewer)
utility.electricProvider: Columbia Power & Water Systems (CPWS) [primary in Spring Hill]; also Middle Tennessee Electric (MTEMC) and Duck River Electric (DREMC) in some areas
utility.tapFees: New service connection fee: $50 (non-refundable, per-account setup). Water tap fees for irrigation meters: $1,300. Development/impact fees vary by property type. Annual rate increases adopted through 2026 (25-30% cumulative over 3 years starting July 2024). Commercial stormwater fee: $6.50/ERU as of July 2024, increasing $0.25-$0.75 annually through 2026.

access.vpdSource: TDOT TCDS accessible at https://tdot.public.ms2soft.com/tcds — requires direct site query by county and station number

utility.fireFlowNotes: No published fire flow or hydrant data in results. Contact CCU Engineering at (941) 764-4300 Option 3 for fire flow requirements and nearest hydrant location.
utility.tapFees: Water $6,792 (current 2025) including TAP fee ($531), Distribution extension ($2,940), Service line ($1,070), Meter ($375); Sewer $11,201 (current 2025). Indexed proposals available. Contact CCU for exact non-MSBU charges.

waterContact.name: Public Works & Utilities Office / Water Department Superintendent (Jim Lallo)

utility.fireFlowNotes: Municipal water required for commercial fire suppression per NJ code; fire hydrants available in developed areas. Exact hydrant locations and main size should be verified with Public Works. Property may require fire line connection per township regulations.
utility.sewerProvider: Burlington Township Department of Public Works & Utilities (sewer available in limited service areas; septic viable elsewhere)
utility.tapFees: Published fee schedule not located in web search; contact Burlington Township Public Works at (609) 239-5909 for current water/sewer connection fees and impact charges.

utility.waterProvider: City of Escondido Water & Sewer Department (Utilities Division)

waterContact.name: City of Escondido Utilities Division / Water Division

utility.fireFlowNotes: Fire flow test available for $500; City maintains approximately 423 miles of pipelines with 50 pressure reducing/regulating stations, 5 pump stations, and 11 reservoirs for reliable water delivery and fire protection capacity.
utility.sewerProvider: City of Escondido Water & Sewer Department
utility.tapFees: Water connection (5/8"-3/4" meter): $4,690 + $6,501 SDCWA capacity charge = $11,191 · Wastewater connection (commercial/industrial per EDU): $7,500 · Water meter installation varies by size ($7,000-$14,400 as of Sept 2024). All fees effective as of January 1, 2026 or September 15, 2024.

access.nearestSignal: unknown — multiple signals on Mission Ave in downtown Escondido area

utility.waterProvider: City of Pflugerville Utilities (primary) — or potentially Paseo Del Este MUD, Texas Water Utilities, or SW Water Co. depending on exact site location
utility.fireFlowNotes: Fire suppression systems required by municipal code; contact City of Pflugerville Utility Engineering at (512) 990-6101 for main size and nearest hydrant location near Pflugerville Pkwy
utility.tapFees: Water and wastewater impact fees established per City Ordinance; specific amounts require consultation with Utility Billing Dept. Recent rate increases noted (FY 2025-26): base fees increased $5–$13.50 depending on meter size, volume charges $0.55–$0.80 per 1,000 gal tier increase for water; wastewater base $4 increase.
